title: Context-Contrastive Uncertainty Decomposition for Reliable Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: We propose C2UD, a method that decomposes RAG uncertainty into three interpretable components -- retrieval sensitivity, context discrimination, and parametric agreement -- by comparing an LLM's behavior under three controlled conditions: with relevant context, without context, and with irrelevant context. The three-condition design enables a Context Discrimination metric that detects indiscriminate context sensitivity, a failure mode invisible to two-condition methods like CRUX. A lightweight logistic regression calibration step combines these components for selective prediction.
motivation: Recent axiomatic analysis (Soudani et al., 2025) has formally shown that existing uncertainty estimation methods systematically fail in RAG settings because they produce a single monolithic score that conflates retrieval failures, grounding failures, and parametric overrides. While concurrent work has begun addressing context-awareness -- CRUX (Yuan et al., 2026) compares with/without context, and FRANQ (Fadeeva et al., 2025) conditions on faithfulness -- these two-condition approaches cannot distinguish genuine grounding from indiscriminate context sensitivity. A model that shifts its answer whenever any document is provided (relevant or not) will appear well-grounded to a two-condition method, but is actually unreliable.
proposed_approach: C2UD generates responses under three controlled conditions: (1) with retrieved documents, (2) without documents (parametric-only), and (3) with irrelevant control documents. From these three conditions, we extract: Retrieval Sensitivity (sequence-level JSD between with-context and no-context distributions), Context Discrimination (normalized ratio of JSD values that measures differential sensitivity to relevant vs. irrelevant context, bounded in [0,1]), and Parametric Agreement (NLI-based semantic agreement between RAG and parametric answers). These components are combined via lightweight logistic regression calibration for selective prediction, or used independently for targeted interventions. The decomposition itself is training-free; only the final aggregation requires a small labeled calibration set (~200 examples).

related_work.title: A Context-Aware Dual-Metric Framework for Confidence Estimation in Large Language Models (CRUX)
related_work.relation: Most closely related work. CRUX uses two conditions (with/without context) to compute contextual entropy reduction and unified consistency. C2UD differs by adding a third irrelevant-context condition that enables the Context Discrimination metric, using sequence-level JSD instead of sampling-based entropy, and explicitly decomposing into three diagnostic components. We include CRUX as a baseline.

related_work.title: Faithfulness-Aware Uncertainty Quantification for Fact-Checking the Output of Retrieval Augmented Generation (FRANQ)
related_work.authors: Fadeeva, Rubashevskii, Piatrashyn, Vashurin, Dhuliawala, Shelmanov, Baldwin, Nakov, Sachan, Panov
related_work.relation: Decomposes RAG uncertainty conditioned on faithfulness (P(true) = P(faithful)*UQ_faith + P(unfaithful)*UQ_unfaith). Operates post-hoc at claim level, while C2UD operates at the generation-process level by controlling input conditions before generation.

related_work.title: Why Uncertainty Estimation Methods Fall Short in RAG: An Axiomatic Analysis
related_work.relation: Identifies the core problem we address: existing UE methods fail in RAG settings. Proposes axioms and a calibration fix but not a principled decomposition.

related_work.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Large Language Models Using Semantic Entropy
related_work.relation: State-of-the-art uncertainty method for LLMs via semantic clustering. Not designed for RAG-specific interactions between context and generation.

related_work.title: Semantic Entropy Probes: Robust and Cheap Hallucination Detection in LLMs
related_work.relation: Efficient hidden-state probes that approximate semantic entropy. Suffers from OOD degradation and is not RAG-aware.

related_work.title: SPUQ: Perturbation-Based Uncertainty Quantification for Large Language Models
related_work.relation: Uses prompt perturbation for epistemic uncertainty. Operates at the prompt level, not the context level, missing RAG-specific signals.

related_work.title: Trusting Your Evidence: Hallucinate Less with Context-Aware Decoding
related_work.authors: Shi, Han, Lewis, Tsvetkov, Zettlemoyer, Yih
related_work.relation: Contrasts with/without context for decoding. We extend this principle from decoding to uncertainty estimation with a third control condition.

related_work.title: Controlling Risk of Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Counterfactual Prompting Framework
related_work.relation: Uses counterfactual variations for RAG risk control. Does not decompose uncertainty into specific components or enable targeted interventions.

related_work.title: When Not to Trust Language Models: Investigating Effectiveness of Parametric and Non-Parametric Memories
related_work.relation: Introduces PopQA and studies when parametric vs. retrieval memory is reliable. Motivates our investigation of parametric agreement as an uncertainty signal.

hypothesis: Decomposing RAG uncertainty into retrieval sensitivity, context discrimination, and parametric agreement -- enabled by a three-condition contrastive design with irrelevant context control -- will yield statistically significantly better selective prediction (measured by AUROC) than both holistic uncertainty measures and two-condition contrastive methods (CRUX) across standard QA benchmarks, while also enabling targeted failure-mode diagnosis.
success_criteria: C2UD achieves statistically significant AUROC improvement (p < 0.05, paired bootstrap) over CRUX and other baselines on at least 2 of 3 datasets (NQ, TriviaQA, PopQA)
Ablation shows the three-condition C2UD-full outperforms the two-condition C2UD-RS+PA ablation, validating the value of the irrelevant context condition
Each component (RS, CD, PA) contributes to performance, with no single component dominating
Decomposed scores correctly identify failure modes on a manually annotated subset of 100 queries
C2UD maintains competitive performance across all three model families (Llama, Mistral, Phi-3)

feasibility.compute: 3 forward passes per query for C2UD. ~27K forward passes across all conditions. NLI model (DeBERTa) is lightweight. Logistic regression calibration is negligible. Total estimated: ~6-7 GPU-hours on 1x A6000.
feasibility.data: All datasets are publicly available. Wikipedia corpus for retrieval available via HuggingFace. Calibration set of ~200 labeled examples drawn from training splits.
feasibility.implementation: Uses HuggingFace transformers, standard retrieval pipeline (Contriever), off-the-shelf NLI model. Logistic regression via scikit-learn. CRUX baseline reimplemented from paper description.
